visualGoal: Make an iRacer want the 9-12Nm sweet-spot base + the right rim before they overspend on torque they'll never use. Real wheelbase product proof throughout; no procedural art. Reinforce that the rim (rotaries/screen) matters as much as the base.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic PC sim-racing bay at cool blue-and-amber rim light, a rigid 8020 wheel-stand with an empty 4-bolt mounting plate left open for a real wheelbase product overlay, a triple-monitor iRacing session glowing on screens (no readable UI), curator Duke Alvarez leaning in with a rotary-dial rim in hand, no fake branded hardware, 16:9 editorial cover.

infographicPrompts.title: How much torque iRacing actually asks for
content: Max Force 35-45Nm = telemetry scaling, NOT hardware
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, racing colors, real base photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: GT rim vs formula rim for iRacing
content: Round GT rim: best all-rounder, rotaries for bias/TC/fuel, mixed-class
Flat formula rim: open-wheel only, display + thumb matrix, worse all-rounder
infographicPrompts.style: Split-panel comparison, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays
